LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE JUN-23-2021 HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS JAMMIE LUE AND JEREMY LUE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS JAMMIE LUE AND JEREMY LUE'S UNOPPOSED SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION IS GRANTED. COUNSEL FOR THE MOVING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH STATES THAT THE MOTION WAS NOT OPPOSED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment
Off Calendar Jun-24-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS JAMMIE LUE AND JEREMY LUE'S Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling: Defendants Jammie Chan Lue and Jeremy Lue's motion to set aside the default entered against them on February 13, 2019 and the default judgment entered against them on August 29, 2019, is granted, on the basis of lack of actual notice.

"When service of a summons has not resulted in actual notice to a party in time to defend the action and a default or default judgment has been entered against him or her in the action, he or she may serve and file a notice of motion to set aside the default or default judgment and for leave to defend the action." (Code Civ. Proc. 473.5.) Defendants testify that they did not become aware of the lawsuit until March 17, 2020, when their insurer contacted them. (Jammie Chan Lue Decl. ISO Mot., 15; Jeremy Lue Decl. ISO Mot., 15.) Plaintiff does not provide any admissible evidence that controverts Defendants' declarations that purported substituted service on December 5, 2018 did not result in actual notice. Plaintiff has not shown that defendants' lack of actual notice was caused by inexcusable neglect or avoidance of service. Plaintiff's request for costs and attorneys' fees is denied.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-27-2019 Off Calendar

Plaintiffs Motion Requesting A Default Judgment Against Defendants Hubert Mendoza, Jammie Chan Lue, Jeremy Lue, Loy Moncada, And Jose Mendoza
Off Calendar Aug-29-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendants Herbert Mendoza, Jammie Chan Lue, Jeremy Lu, Loy Moncada and Jose Mendoza.
Dept. 514. Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-717-9768. Email: casey.giselle@gmail.com.

Nikolaus W. Reed, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Christian Vivero, who was placed under oath and testified.
No appearance by defendants Herbert Mendoza, Jammie Chan Lue, Jeremy Lu, Loy Moncada of Jose Mendoza.
Plaintiff's complaint for Personal Injury/Negligence (Dog Bite) was filed on October 15, 2019, and personally served on defendant Loy Moncada on November 21, 2019.
Substitute service on defendants Herbert Memdoza, Jammie Chan Lue, Jeremy Lue and Jose Mendoza was made on December 4, 2018 and was on effectuated on December 15, 2018.
Statement of Damages as to each of the defendants was served with the Summons & Complaint.
Default as to all five defendants was entered on February 13, 2019.
DOE defendants were dismissed today, August 29, 2019.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Christian Vivero, and against the default defendants Herbert Mendoza, Jammie Chan Lue, Jeremy Lu, Loy Moncada and Jose Mendoza, as follows:

General Damages - $50,000.00.
Medical Expenses - $4,867.50.
Costs - $1,049.10.

Total - $55,916.60.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
